Since ST is parallel to UV => angle USR = 50°(alternate to VUS.
Angle UST = 180° - 50° = 130° (angle on a straight line)

question: If the mean of the numbers 0, (x+2), (3x+6), and (4x+8) is 4, find their mean deviation.
solution:
Mean = {0 + (x+2) + (3x+6) + (4x+8)}/4 = 4
=> 0 + (x+2) + (3x+6) + (4x+8) = 16
8x + 16 = 16
x = 0
Now prepare a table showing the deviation of each of 0, (x+2), (3x+6) and (4x+8), adding the deviations will give 12.
Thus M.D = 12/4 = 3

question: Find the number of sides of a regular polygon whose interior angle is twice the exterior angle.
solution:
Let the ext. angle = x
Thus int. angle = 2x
But sum of int + ext = 180 (angle of a straight line).
2x + x = 180
3x = 180
x = 180/3 = 60

Each ext angle = 360/n
=> 60 = 360/n
n = 360/60 = 6

question: P(-6, 1) and Q(6, 6) are the two ends of the diameter of a given circle. Calculate the radius.
solution:
PQ<sup>2</sup> = (x<sub>2</sub> - x<sub>1</sub>)<sup>2</sup> + (y<sub>2</sub> - y<sub>1</sub>)<sup>2</sup>

= 12<sup>2</sup> + 5<sup>2</sup>
= 144 + 25
= 169

PQ = √169 = 13

But PQ = diameter = 2r, r = PQ/2 = 6.5 units

question: Find the area bounded by the curves y = 4 - x<sup>2</sup> and y = 2x + 1
solution:
Hint:
y = 4 - x<sup>2</sup> and y = 2x + 1
=> 4 - x<sup>2</sup> = 2x + 1
=> x<sup>2</sup> + 2x - 3 = 0
(x+3)(x-1) = 0
thus x = -3 or x = 1.

Integrating x<sup>2</sup> + 2x - 3 from (-3, to 1) w.r.t x will give 31/3 = 10(1/3)

In the diagram above, a cylinder is summounted by a hemisphere bowl. Calculate the volume of the solid.
solution:
Volume of cylinder = πr<sup>2</sup>h
= π x 3<sup>2</sup> x 20
= π x 9 x 20
= 180 πcm<sup>3</sup>
Volume of hemisphere = 2/3πr<sup>3</sup>
= 2/3 x π x 3<sup>2</sup>
= 2 x π x 3<sup>2</sup>
= 2 x π x 9
= 18π
= Volume of the solid = 180π x 18π
= 198πcm<sup>3</sup>

question: The diagram above is the graph of the function f(x). Determined the range of values of x for which f(x) ≤ 0
solution: The graph of the function f(x) is a curve that starts at (-2,0) and ends at (2,0) with a peak at (0,1). The range of values of x for which f(x) ≥ 0 is the interval where the graph of the function is above or touches the x-axis. From the given graph, we can see that this interval is [-2, 2]. Therefore, the correct answer is -2 ≤ x ≤ 0, x ≥ 2

question:
In the diagram above, O is the center of the circle,

∠UOT = 70° and ∠RST = 100°. Calculate ∠RUO.
solution:
∠OUT = ∠OTU = a (Base ∠s of 180 Δ)
∴ a + a + 70 = 180° (sum of ∠s of a Δ)
2a = 180° - 70°
2a = 110°
a = 55°
But ∠RUT + ∠RBT = 180° (opposite ∠s of a Cyclic quad)
∴ x + a = 100 = 180
x + 55 + 100 = 180
x = 180 - 155
x = 25
so ∠RUO = x = 25°

question: In the diagram above calculate the value of x
solution:
p + 40° = 100° (exterior ∠ = sum of two interior opp ∠s)
p = 100° - 40°
P = 60°
But q + p = 180° (∠s on a straight line)
q + 60°
q = 180° - 60°
q = 120°
x = q (corresponding ∠)
∴x = 120°

If the lines 2y - kx + 2 = 0 and y + x - k/2 = 0 Intersect at (1, -2), find the value of k
solution:
If the point of intersection is (1, -2), it implies that x = 1 and y = -2 when the two equation are solved simultaneously.
∴ substitute x = 1 and y = -2 in any of the equations
2y - k x + 2 = 0
2(-2) - k(1) + 2 = 0
-4 - k + 2 = 0
-4 + 2 = k
-2 = k

Differentiate sin x - x cos x
solution:
sin x - x cos x

= co x - [cos x - x sin x]
= cos x - cos x + x sin x
= x sin x

A circular arc subtends angle 150° at the centre of a circle of radius 12cm. Calculate the area of the sector of the arc.
solution:

= 150/360 × π × 12<sup>2</sup>
= 60π cm<sup>2</sup>

question: Calculate the median age of the frequency distribution in the table above
solution:
N = ∑f = 15

Hence the median age is the (N+1/2)th age,i.e.

(15+1)/2)th = 8th

From the table, the age that falls on the 8th position when arranged in ascending order is 25years

question: In triangle PQR, q = 8 cm, r = 6 cm and cos P = 1/12 Calculate the value of
solution:
Using the cosine rule, we have

p<sup>2</sup> = q<sup>2</sup> + r<sup>2</sup> − 2qrcosP
p<sup>2</sup> = 8<sup>2</sup> + 6<sup>2</sup> − 2(8)(6)(1/12)
= 64 + 36 − 8
p<sup>2</sup> = 92∴ p = √9cm

Find the range of the following set of numbers 0.4, −0.4, 0.3, 0.47, −0.53, 0.2 and −0.2
solution:
0.4, −0.4, 0.3, 0.47, −0.53, 0.2, −0.2

Range is the difference between the highest and lowest value

i.e Highest − Lowest

− 0.53, −0.4, −0.2, 0.2, 0.3, 0.4, 0.47

0.47 is the highest

− 0.53 is the lowest

∴ = 0.47 − (− 0.53)

∴0.47 + 0.53

= 1.0

question: From the diagram above, find the value of x

question: The probabilities that John and James pass an examination are 3/4 and 3/5 respectively. Find the probability of both boys failing the examination.
solution:
Pr(both John and James passed)

= 3/4 x 3/5 = 9/20
Pr(john and james failed)= 1-Pr(john and james passed)
= 1 – 920
= 11/20

A machine valued at N20,000 depreciates by 10% every year. What will be the value of the machine at the end of two years?
solution:
Since it depreciates by 10% At the end of first year, its value = 90% of 20000

= 90/100
 x 20000 =18000

  At the end of second year, its value = 90% of 18000

  = 90/100
 x 18000 = ₦16,200

question: Find the equation of the line through (5,7) parallel to the line 7x + 5y = 12
In the figure below, /MX/ = 8cm, /XN/ = 12cm, /NZ/ = 4cm and ∠ XMN = ∠ XZY. Calculate /YM/
solution:
From the figure,

  ∠ XMN = ∠ XZY

  Angle X is common

  So, ∠ XNM = ∠ XYZ

Then from the angle relationship

XM/XZ = XN/XY = MN/ZY
XM = 8, XZ = 12 + 4 = 16,

XN = 12, XY = 8 + YM

8/16 = 12/(8+YM)
Cross multiply

 8(8 + YM) = 192

 64 + 8YM = 192

 8YM = 128

 YM = 128/8
 = 16cm

question: What is the loci of a distance 4cm from a given point P?
solution: Locus is the path traced at by a point which moves in accordance with a certain law. It is also the set of all possible position occupied by an object The path traced from all possible location of 4cm from a given point P form a circle of radius 4cm with centre P.

= 2(3)sin(68/2)
= 6sin 34
= 6 × 0.559
= 3.354 cm ≊

question: The locus of a point which moves so that it is equidistant from two intersecting straight lines is the

option.b: line parallel to the two lines
option.c: angle bisector of the two lines
option.d: perpendicular bisector of the two lines

solution: The locus of a points equidistant from two intersecting straight lines is a pair of bisectors that bisect the angles formed by the two intersecting lines.

The locus of a point which moves so that it is equidistant from two intersecting straight lines is the?

option.a: perpendicular bisector of the two lines
option.b: angle bisector of the two lines
option.d: line parallel to the two lines

solution: The required locus is angle bisector of the two lines

solution:
sample space = 16

sum of nos. removed are (2), 3, (4), 5

3, (4), 5, (6)

(4), 5, (6), 7

(5), 6, 7, (8)

Even nos. = 8 of them

Pr(even sum) = 8/16


= 1/2

question: A car travels from calabar to Enugu, a distance of P km with an average speed of U km per hour and continues to benin, a distance of Q km, with an average speed of Wkm per hour. Find its average speed from Calabar to Benin
solution:
Average speed = totalDistance/TotalTime


from Calabar to Enugu in time t1, hence

t1 = P/U
Also from Enugu to Benin

t2 = q/w


Av. speed = (p + q)/(t1 + t2)


= (p + q)/((p/u) + (q/w))


= p + q x (uw)/(pw + qu)


= (uw(p+q))/(pw + qu)

Mr Adu spends his annual salary on food(f), rent(r), car maintenance, gift+B10s(g), savings(s) and some B28miscellaneous (m) as indicate in the table below:                 If the above information is represented on a pie chart. What angle represents his spending on food?
In how many ways can the letter of ZOOLOGY be arranged?
solution:
Zoology has 7 letters in total, with O repeated thrice

7!/3!
 → 7∗6∗5∗4∗3∗2∗1/3∗2∗1
= 840ways

question:
What will be the result obtained when the numerator of 96/50
 is decreased by 37.5% and its denominator decreased by 20%.
solution:
Numerator: 96 → 37.5% of 96 = 36

Decreased by 36 → 96 - 36

New numerator = 60

 

Denominator: 50 → 20% of 50 = 10

Decreased by 10 → 50 - 10 = 40

New Denominator = 40

 

New fraction = 60/40
 or 1.5

question: Three times a certain number (x), minus 2 is less than the number minus 6.Find the possible values of x.
solution:
Three times a certain number (x) = 3x

minus 2 = -2

is less than = <

the number minus 6 = x -6

Mathematically; 3x - 2 < x - 6

collect like terms

3x - x < -6 + 2

2x < -4

x < -2 is  the possible values of x

question: Evaluate the following limit: lim <sub>(x→2)</sub> (x<sup>2</sup> + 4x − 12)/(x<sup>2</sup> − 2x)
Factorize: 16x<sup>4</sup>−y<sup>4</sup>
solution:
16x<sup>4</sup> − y<sup>4</sup>


= 2<sup>4</sup>x<sup>4</sup> − y<sup>4</sup>


= (2x)<sup>4</sup> − y<sup>4</sup>


= ((2x)<sup>2</sup>)<sup>2</sup> − (y<sup>2</sup>)<sup>2</sup>


Using a<sup>2</sup> − b<sup>2</sup> = (a - b)(a + b) identity

= ((2x)<sup>2</sup>−y<sup>2</sup>)((2x)<sup>2</sup>+y<sup>2</sup>)


Using the identity one more time

= (2x − y)(2x + y)((2x)<sup>2</sup> + y<sup>2</sup>)


∴ (2x − y)(2x + y)(4x<sup>2</sup> + y<sup>2</sup>)

question: Two numbers are respectively 35% and 80% more than a third number. The ratio of the two numbers is
solution:
Let the third number = x
Then the first number = 100% x + 35%x = 135x/100 = 1.35x (Note: 100% x=x)

The second number = 180% x = 180x/100 = 1.80x
∴ The ratio of the first number to the second number = 1.35x:1.80x=3:4

Find x if 132<sub>x</sub>  = 70 <sub>eight</sub> .
solution:
132<sub>x</sub> = 70<sub>8</sub>

1 × x<sup>2</sup> + 3 × x<sup>1</sup> + 2 × x<sup>0</sup> 


7 × 8<sup>1</sup> + 0 ×8<sup>0</sup> 


x<sup>2</sup> +3x + 2 −56 = 0


x<sup>2</sup> + 3x −54 = 0


x(x + 9) −6(x + 9) = 0

(x + 9)(x − 6) = 0

Either (x + 9) = 0 or (x − 6) = 0

x = − 9 or +6

The positive values for x = 6

The base number for 132<sub>x</sub>  = 132<sub>6</sub>

question: A man sells his new brand car for N420,000 at a gain of 15%. What did it cost him?
solution:
Cost Price:

Selling price = 100 : (100 + 15)

= 100 : 115

Cost Price

= 100 ÷ 115 of selling price (i.e N420,000)

= 100 ÷ 115 × N420,000

= N365,217.4

∴ Cost Price = N365, 217
